justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and thoroughly. Formal escalation produced with explicit 'ESCALATE TO EXECUTIVE LEADERSHIP' recommendation. No acceptance criteria produced (empty array). Confidence level is 30, well under the 45 threshold. A compromise (zone-level/ETA-based tracking) is proposed as Option C and clearly labeled as a 'starting hypothesis' requiring executive arbitration, not presented as a resolution.

observations.text: Excellent stakeholder characterization — both Customer Success and Operations interests are described with empathy and business grounding, neither dismissed
Strong formal escalation with four concrete options (A/B/C/D) and explicit recommendation to escalate to executive leadership
Acceptance criteria array is empty, correctly refusing to produce criteria for an unresolved conflict
Confidence level of 30 with thorough reasoning explaining why it cannot be higher
Identified a missing stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) and raised it as a separate escalation with a blocking recommendation
Edge cases are documented but each explicitly notes 'Cannot define without resolving the core conflict'
Rejection reasons clearly articulate why the spec cannot proceed as-is
Business impact section quantifies risks on both sides rather than favoring one perspective

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being specified for customer-facing delivery visibility. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements regarding whether courier GPS location should be shown to customers during delivery. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved by technical design alone — it requires executive arbitration.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Customer Visibility","ubiquitous

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'contradiction', got values: 'missing_context'
